name: Navaluenga and Embalse del Burguillo Loop from Navalmoral
name_original: Navaluenga, embalse del Burguillo y San Juan de la Nava — circular desde Navalmoral
description: This moderate 26.8-mile (43.2 km) mountain bike route from Navalmoral circles Embalse del Burguillo, offering varied terrain and scenic view
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Navalmoral de la Mata offer diverse terrain across the Campo Arañuelo region of Extremadura. The landscape features expansive meadows, plains, and pine forests, providing varied routes for mountain biking. Proximity to Monfragüe National Park allows for rides through oak and holm oak pastures, while the Tagus River and Valdecañas Reservoir offer scenic riverine paths. This area provides a mix of natural beauty and historical landmarks for cyclists to explore. Best mountain bike trails around Navalmoral The most popular mountain bike trail is Navaluenga and Embalse del Burguillo Loop from Navalmoral, a 26.8 miles (43.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route offers varied terrain through the region's natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop from Parking del mirador estelar de Navaluenga, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path. This route winds through scenic areas, including views of the Tagus River. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Alberche River – Navaluenga Valley loop from Parking del mirador estelar de Navaluenga, a 23.0 miles (36.9 km) trail leading through river valleys and pine forests, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Navalmoral is defined by meadows, pine forests, and riverine paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Navalmoral are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navalmoral's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Navalmoral de la Mata, offering diverse terrain across the Campo Arañuelo region. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging paths.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in Navalmoral de la Mata are rated as moderate, with 31 out of 43 routes falling into this category. There are also 3 easy trails and 9 difficult trails, providing options for different experience levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking near Navalmoral?
A: You can expect to ride through a variety of landscapes, including expansive meadows, plains, and pine forests typical of the Campo Arañuelo region. Many routes also offer scenic views of the Tagus River and the Valdecañas Reservoir, and some extend towards the oak and holm oak pastures near Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: Yes, there are a few easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. While specific family-designated routes are not detailed, the region's varied terrain includes gentler paths through meadows and plains that can be enjoyed by riders of all ages. It's always recommended to check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see on mountain bike routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. For instance, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4268792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5678472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alberche River&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Burguillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The mountain bike trails in Navalmoral de la Mata are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic riverine paths, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Navalmoral de la Mata are circular. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087852978/navaluenga-and-embalse-del-burguillo-loop-from-navalmoral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga and Embalse del Burguillo Loop from Navalmoral&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular 43.2 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alberche River – Navaluenga Valley loop from Parking del mirador estelar de Navaluenga&lt;/a&gt;, a 36.9 km route through river valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navalmoral de la Mata?
A: The region experiences hot summers, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable. The varied terrain means trails can be enjoyed across different seasons, adapting to the weather.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific regulations for each route, especially if they pass through protected areas like Monfragüe National Park where rules for pets might be stricter. Generally, dogs on a leash are welcome on many public paths, but ensure they are comfortable with the distance and terrain.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on mountain bike trails around Navalmoral?
A: Elevation gains vary significantly depending on the trail. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087852978/navaluenga-and-embalse-del-burguillo-loop-from-navalmoral" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navaluenga and Embalse del Burguillo Loop from Navalmoral&lt;/a&gt; involves an ascent of over 700 meters, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21952413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga – View of the Alberche River loop from Burgohondo&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of around 300 meters. This variety allows riders to choose routes that match their fitness levels.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for mountain biking in Navalmoral?
A: Parking availability can vary by trailhead. Many routes, especially those starting from towns or popular recreational areas, will have designated parking. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33816749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Viejo de Navaluenga loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from Parking del mirador estelar de Navaluenga, indicating specific parking facilities. It's advisable to check the starting point details for each route on komoot for precise parking information.
tips: This old stone bridge is located in a beautiful setting in the town of Navaluenga. The bridge allows you to cross the Alberche River and divides the two bathing areas of the Navaluenga natural pools during the summer months. An ideal place to add to my route.
Beautiful views that I find when I reach this point. A natural viewpoint of the Alberche river that is undoubtedly worth visiting.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 43.2 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete on a mountain bike. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It demands a good level of physical fitness due to its length and a considerable elevation gain of about 708 meters. While not for absolute beginners, experienced riders will find it a rewarding challenge.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail that starts and finishes in Navalmoral. While specific parking details aren't provided, Navalmoral is the designated starting point, suggesting parking options would be available within or on the outskirts of the town.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, including approximately 30.5 km of paths, 9.04 km of paved road, 2.33 km of side roads, and a dedicated 1.35 km of singletrack. This mix offers a diverse and engaging riding experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views, particularly as it circles the Embalse del Burguillo (Burguillo reservoir). You'll pass by the historic Puente Viejo de Navaluenga and enjoy a View of the Alberche River. Keep an eye out for potential vistas of the 'Isla del Castillo del Bur...' (Island of the Castle of Bur...) as well.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Ávila province of Castilla y León, Spain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, while winter might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Be prepared to carry water for your dog, as natural water sources might be seasonal or not always accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this route?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required to access or ride this specific mountain bike route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or water sources near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and finishes in Navalmoral, you can expect to find amenities like cafes and shops in the town. Along the trail, especially around the Embalse del Burguillo and through other villages, there might be limited options, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike ride, wear appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, padded shorts, and layers suitable for the weather. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and a charged phone. Sun protection is also recommended.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the [CIMA AV03] Navalmoral, Barranco Cambronal, Camino de Piedralaves, and the Puente Viejo trails, adding to the varied experience.
